meaning: n.自愿捐助主义,实行志愿兵役制的主张,唯意志论
examples: Yet the AFL's voluntarism had accommodated certain exceptions: the AFL had supported government intervention on behalf of injured workers and child laborers.
The problematic part of the agenda is voluntarism.
Faith used to drive a lot of voluntarism, civic engagement and self-reliance.
Now that voluntarism in Japan is suddenly on the increase, the government cannot resist regulating it.
As with FCPA, such voluntarism shows maximum institutional commitment and can thereby serve to soften whatever prosecutorial blows the government may need to deliver.
